description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped, curved stone barrier composed of several large, roughly hewn rocks joined together, with a surface covered in patches of green moss and lichen. The stones are uneven in size and shape, with some protruding more than others, creating a rugged, natural appearance. The top surface is mostly flat but not level, with visible depressions and raised areas, suggesting it could have been shaped by weathering or human placement. The coloration is predominantly gray with earthy brown and green tones, indicating exposure to moisture and organic growth. The structure appears solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or openings, and the texture is coarse and granular. This type of stone barrier is commonly found in urban parks, along pathways, or as part of landscaping features in public spaces.
description: A curved, rugged stone barrier with mossy patches and a coarse, weathered surface, composed of several large, irregular rocks. Its coloration is mainly gray with earthy and green tones, giving it a natural, aged look.
description_view_0: From this angle, the stone's curved shape and the prominent mossy patches on the larger rocks are clearly visible, highlighting its uneven, stepped profile.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the elongated, low profile of the barrier, showing the flatness of the top surface and the distribution of stones along its length.
description_view_2: Here, the curvature is most apparent, with the largest stone sections facing forward and the moss coverage more pronounced on the upper surfaces.
description_view_3: This view reveals the opposite side, where the stones appear more compact and the moss is less dense, with the rough, granular texture of the stone more visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature along the edge of a walkway or garden
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling from moss and lichen, with natural stone veining and rough, granular texture; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough with patches of moss and lichen, indicating significant weathering and prolonged exposure to moisture and natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with landscaped environments, partially covered by vegetation and soil, visually integrating with adjacent greenery and earth.
